submit.title: Innovation Director
submit.jd_text:
The Innovation Director leads the company’s innovation and transformation agenda, driving the development and execution of high-impact initiatives aligned with strategic objectives. This role focuses on fostering a culture of innovation, adopting emerging technologies, and enabling sustainable transformation, in line with Beyond Company’s mission to enhance institutional performance across public and private sectors.
Key Responsibilities:
Develop and lead the organization’s innovation strategy aligned with business goals.Design and implement innovation and transformation initiatives, leveraging modern technologies.Oversee the full lifecycle of innovation initiatives from ideation to execution and evaluation.Establish innovation frameworks, methodologies, and performance measurement tools to ensure effectiveness and scalability.Foster a culture of creativity, experimentation, and continuous improvement across the organization.Collaborate with government entities and strategic partners to develop innovative solutions aligned with Saudi Vision 2030.Monitor innovation performance using KPIs and provide regular insights and reports to executive leadership.Identify emerging trends, technologies, and opportunities that can drive business growth and transformation.Represent the company in innovation-related events, forums, and industry engagements.
Qualifications and Experience:
Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field; Master’s degree preferred.10–12 years of experience in innovation, transformation, or strategic program leadership.Experience working with government entities or leading consulting firms is highly preferred.Strong knowledge of innovation management, digital transformation, and emerging technologies.Professional certifications such as Design Thinking or Innovation Management are preferred.Exceptional leadership, analytical, and communication skills with the ability to influence and engage stakeholders at all levels.

submit.title: Head of Transformation and Strategy
submit.jd_text:
We have partnered with a large, diversified UAE-based conglomerate to appoint a Head of Transformation. This is a Group-level leadership role responsible for designing and driving a coordinated business transformation agenda across a complex, multi-sector organisation. Reporting directly to the Group CEO, this is a rare opportunity to shape how a major conglomerate evolves its operating model, organisational structure and commercial performance at scale.
This role is for someone who has led large, complex business transformation programmes from strategy through to execution, not IT or digital change, but genuine enterprise-wide transformation spanning people, process, structure and culture. You will be a credible, senior voice in the room, capable of influencing at the highest levels while maintaining hands-on accountability for delivery.
About the role:Define and lead the Group's business transformation strategy, ensuring alignment with the overall vision and priorities of Group leadershipIdentify and drive transformation opportunities across business units, including operating model redesign, organisational effectiveness, process improvement and commercial performanceBuild and lead a high-performing transformation function, embedding structured programme delivery and change management disciplines across the GroupOwn the transformation portfolio from business case through to benefits realisation, maintaining clear accountability and pace throughoutAct as a trusted advisor to the Group CEO, COO and Board, providing transparent visibility on progress, risks and outcomesPartner with business unit CEOs and functional leaders to build transformation capability and drive sustained adoption of changeEstablish governance frameworks and reporting mechanisms that give leadership confidence in the execution of the transformation agendaDrive a performance culture within the transformation function, setting high standards for rigour, commercial thinking and delivery quality
 About you:15+ years of progressive experience leading business transformation within large, complex, multi-business-unit organisations or a minimum of 3 years as a Principal or Senior Engagement Manager at a Tier 1 management consultancy with direct accountability on large-scale business transformation mandatesProven track record of delivering enterprise-wide transformation programmes spanning operating model change, organisational redesign and performance improvementDeep understanding of the structural and operational dynamics of diversified conglomerates, including navigating matrix environments, multiple P&Ls and competing stakeholder agendasStrong commercial acumen with the ability to connect transformation activity to tangible business outcomes and financial value
submit.verdict_reason: Perfect strategic fit - Group-level transformation, reports to CEO, operating model redesign, multi-sector conglomerate, target GCC geography
ats_gaps: Missing: digital transformation, project management, program management

submit.title: Director of Fintech
submit.jd_text:
HungerStation is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

To lead and oversee HS’s efforts to use technology, in order to drive the development and implementation of financial technology solutions to enhance financial processes, customer transactions, and overall business efficiency.

Fintech

Develop and execute Hungerstation’s financial technology strategy in alignment with business growth, digital transformation, and customer experience objectives.Lead the design and implementation of scalable fintech solutions to support high-volume transactions across customers, merchants, riders, and internal finance operations.Drive innovation in payment systems, digital wallets, refunds, settlements, and reconciliation processes to ensure speed, accuracy, and reliability.Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Data, Operations, and Finance teams to embed fintech solutions seamlessly into Hungerstation’s platform and operational workflows.Ensure full compliance with Saudi regulatory requirements (SAMA, ZATCA, VAT, data protection) and Delivery Hero group policies, while proactively managing fintech-related risks and controls.Oversee fraud detection, transaction monitoring, and cybersecurity measures related to financial systems and digital payments.Own and manage the fintech budget, ensuring cost efficiency, ROI optimization, and sustainable investment in new technologies and partnerships.Analyze financial and transaction data to identify trends, revenue opportunities, cost optimization areas, and system performance improvements.Prepare executive-level financial technology reports and dashboards, providing insights on transaction performance, risk exposure, and innovation impact.Lead and develop a high-performing fintech team, fostering a culture of ownership, innovation, and continuous improvement in line with Hungerstation’s leadership principles.Stay ahead of emerging fintech trends (open banking, embedded finance, BNPL, digital wallets, AI-driven fraud prevention) and assess their relevance to Hungerstation’s ecosystem.Build and manage strategic relationships with banks, payment providers, fintech startups, and regulatory bodies to strengthen Hungerstation’s financial infrastructure.Support business expansion initiatives by enabling new financial products and services for merchants and customers (e.g., faster settlements, flexible payment options, and value-added financial tools).

Strategy & Planning

Contribute to the development and execution of the Division’s ABCs and OKRs, while ensuring alignment with other Functions' heads.Make critical decisions that support the Division's strategic and tactical direction.Establish the plans for the respective functions/teams and provide inputs that support the Division’s goals and objectives. Distribute tactical responsibilities effectively to potential leaders in the Division, to improve the succession readiness of the organization over time. Measure and track relevant OKRs and metrics to ensure the achievement of goals and take corrective actions when necessary.

Governance and Resilience

Follow all relevant policies, procedures, and processes for the daily work to be carried out in a controlled and consistent manner. Contribute to the identification of opportunities for continuous improvement of processes and practices to enhance effectiveness. Uphold a high-performance working environment and promote HS’s Values.

People Management

Set performance objectives, provide necessary support, evaluate direct reports, and provide regular feedback on performance. Participate in the identification and recruitment of key talents for the function. Provide clear direction, prioritize tasks, assign and delegate responsibility, and monitor the workflow. Provide opportunities for team members to suggest, participate in, and contribute to improvement, innovation, and knowledge-sharing initiatives.

Budget Management

Manage and own the budgeting cycle and provide input to the budgeting process. Ensure effective utilization of the function's budget, and report accurately on progress and challenges. Investigate and propose implementing strategies that result in a positive financial impact for HS.Manage the reporting departments effectively within budget, and report accurately on progress made and challenges encountered. Formulate strategies that result in a positive financial impact for HS and aim to mitigate financial and operational risks.

Qualifications

Knowledge and Experience

10 - 12 years of relevant experience. 

Education and Certifications

Bachelor’s degree in finance, Fintech, or a related field.Master’s degree is Preferred.
submit.verdict_reason: Director-level fintech leadership in target geography (Riyadh); owns fintech strategy, payment systems, digital transformation aligned with e-commerce platform

submit.title: Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation
submit.jd_text:
Remote | Global Role | Multi-Industry

This is not a traditional asset management role

This role is designed for someone who understands that the future of maintenance and asset management is driven by data, reliability engineering, and intelligent systems.

If your experience is rooted in Reliability-Centred Maintenance (RCM), preventive and predictive strategies, labour optimisation, and cost control, and you are interested in applying this knowledge to AI-driven solutions, this is a unique opportunity to operate at the forefront of the industry.

About MaxAccelerate Technology Group

MaxAccelerate Technology Group is a global technology and transformation business focused on helping asset-intensive organisations improve performance, reduce cost, and scale operations through data, systems, and AI.

Through our ecosystem, including DataMax AI, we work with organisations across Facilities Management, Transportation, Pharma, Manufacturing, Energy, and Infrastructure to transform how assets are managed.

Our approach is grounded in real operational understanding:

 Maintenance strategies must be structured, measurable, and optimised  Systems such as IBM Maximo and other EAM platforms must deliver actionable insight  AI must be applied in a way that drives tangible outcomes across cost, labour, and asset performance 

The Role

We are seeking a Head of Asset Management & AI Transformation to lead the definition and delivery of AI-driven asset management solutions.

The focus of the role is to take deep operational knowledge — including:

 RCM frameworks  Preventive and condition-based maintenance strategies  Workforce planning and labour models  Spare parts, inventory, and asset lifecycle considerations 

and translate these into structured AI use cases and scalable solutions.

Key Responsibilities

 Work closely with Heads of Maintenance, Engineering Leaders, and Operations teams to understand:  Maintenance strategies and maturity  Preventive vs reactive ratios  Labour utilisation and workforce efficiency  Spare parts management and inventory control  Define and structure AI use cases that address:  Reduction in reactive maintenance  Optimisation of preventive maintenance schedules  Labour efficiency and workforce planning  Spare parts forecasting and cost reduction  Asset lifecycle optimisation  Develop and lead Proof of Concepts (PoCs) that validate:  Operational improvements  Cost savings  Performance uplift  Translate business requirements into clear scope of works for DataMax AI R&D teams  Ensure AI solutions integrate effectively with EAM/CMMS platforms (Maximo preferred) and align with real operational workflows  Build clear ROI models, ensuring every solution delivers measurable impact across:  Maintenance costs  Asset uptime  Labour efficiency  Inventory and working capital  Support clients in adopting solutions that improve their infrastructure, operations, and long-term asset strategy 

Why This Role Matters

Most organisations have:

 Maintenance strategies defined on paper  Systems implemented but underutilised  Data available but not fully leveraged 

This role exists to bridge that gap.

You will ensure that:

 Maintenance frameworks such as RCM are not just theoretical, but digitised and optimised  Preventive strategies are continuously refined based on real performance data  Labour and cost structures are aligned with actual operational demand  AI is applied in a way that improves real infrastructure outcomes, not just reporting 

What We Are Looking For

 Strong background in Asset Management, Maintenance, or Engineering leadership  Deep understanding of:  RCM and reliability frameworks  Preventive and predictive maintenance strategies  Workforce and labour optimisation  Spare parts and inventory management  Experience working with EAM/CMMS platforms, ideally IBM Maximo  Ability to translate operational challenges into structured, solution-driven approaches  Commercial awareness and the ability to link technical improvements to cost and performance outcomes 

The Opportunity

This role offers the opportunity to work across industries and influence how organisations manage and optimise critical assets.

You will play a key role in:

 Defining how AI is applied to asset management in real environments  Delivering measurable improvements in cost, performance, and efficiency  Shaping solutions that can be scaled across multiple industries 

Apply

If you are interested in moving beyond traditional asset management roles and want to be involved in shaping the next generation of maintenance and asset optimisation, we would welcome a conversation.
submit.verdict_reason: Director-level DT/AI role in target GCC geography with operational excellence alignment, though asset management domain is tangential to core strengths
ats_gaps: Missing: digital transformation, project management, program management

Job Summary : The role is a senior leadership role that focuses on driving digital transformation, developing technology-driven products, and enhance commercial value through digital channels. The role will spearhead the digital transformation of products, driving adoption of digital platforms, automating processes, and enhancing customer experience (user journey) to increase revenue and operational efficiency. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership: Develop and implement a digital strategy for trade solutions that aligns with organizational goals and market trends. Product Management & Commercialization: Lead the development, launch, and enhancement of digital trade channels, such as Port Community Systems (PCS), Supply Chain Finance platforms, and digital customs systems. Onboarding & Client Services: Manage the onboarding of clients and counterparties to digital platforms, driving adoption rates and ensuring a seamless user experience. The Head of Credit Decisioning has end-to-end accountability for the design, governance, and performance of automated credit decision frameworks across consumer and business lending products. The role leads the strategy, development, deployment, and continuous optimization of credit decision engines spanning origination, underwriting, limit management, pricing, and early warning systems, leveraging strong AI driven solutions.
This role is highly strategic and technical, combining advanced analytics, AI and machine learning, decision science, and strong regulatory governance to enable scalable, fast, fair, and profitable credit decisions. The Delivery team are at the epicentre of all projects, ensuring they’re delivered to the highest quality, on time, and that clients receive the greatest possible value from their investment

MBZUAI is seeking an Executive Director to provide overall vision, leadership, and strategic direction for the Institute for Agriculture & AI (IAAI). The Executive Director is responsible for shaping and executing the Institute’s mission at the intersection of artificial intelligence, agriculture, and sustainability. 
The role oversees strategy development, institutional management, partnership building, and resource mobilization, while representing the Institute to funders, government entities, industry partners, and the global research community. The Executive Director ensures the Institute’s long-term impact, relevance, and financial sustainability.

Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional

From the projects you will work on to the clients we partner with. From career‑defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where everyone contributes to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it will be anything but ordinary.

Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional. 

From the projects you will work on to the clients we work with. From career-defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where we all contribute to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it’ll be anything but ordinary.

Your next role

Buro Happold is supporting the transformation of cities and public services across the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the wider GCC, aligned with Vision 2030, national transformation programmes, and public‑sector reform agendas. As Director – Transaction Advisory within our Municipal Services team, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping, structuring, and delivering complex public‑sector transactions across the region.

Based in KSA, you will lead Transaction Advisory services across priority municipal sectors including urban services, mobility, utilities, public realm, mixed-use development, sport and social infrastructure. You will work closely with senior public‑sector stakeholders and delivery entities.

This role offers a rare opportunity to combine deep KSA market understanding, strong knowledge of the local NCP PPP lifecycle framework, and global best practice to help public authorities structure bankable, resilient, and outcome‑driven transactions that deliver long‑term value for citizens.

Your key duties

As a Director, you will provide senior leadership to our Transaction Advisory and PPP offering within Municipal Services, driving growth, credibility, and excellence in delivery across KSA and the GCC.

You will:

Lead the Transaction Advisory strategy in KSA and the GCC for public‑sector projects, aligned with Buro Happold’s regional growth.Originate and lead PPP, privatization, and transaction advisory engagements, including project screening, feasibility studies, business cases, value‑for‑money assessments, and procurement strategies.Advise public‑sector clients across the full PPP lifecycle, including project identification, structuring, market sounding, procurement, bid evaluation, and support through financial close.Act as a trusted advisor to senior government, municipal, and delivery authority leadership, supporting decisions on service delivery models, private‑sector participation, and long‑term operating arrangements.Provide expert advice on commercial, contractual, and risk‑allocation structures, ensuring transactions are bankable, compliant with KSA frameworks, and aligned with policy and affordability objectives.Work closely with multidisciplinary teams (engineering, planning, operations, digital, economics) to deliver integrated, technically robust, and commercially sound transaction solutions.Lead high‑level client engagements, workshops, negotiations, and market‑facing interactions with government entities, investors, developers, operators, and lenders.Drive business development across KSA and the GCC, including strategic client relationships, opportunity origination, bid leadership, and framework participation.Represent Buro Happold with NCP, municipalities, ministries, and key industry stakeholders, strengthening our position as a trusted advisor in PPP and public‑sector transactions.Mentor and develop regional advisory talent, building a strong, locally grounded Transaction Advisory capability within the Municipal Services team.Oversee portfolio performance, ensuring quality assurance, commercial success, and effective risk management across advisory commissions.


Your Skills And Experience

You will bring a combination of regional credibility, transaction expertise, and senior‑level leadership experience.

You will have:

A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related discipline; a postgraduate qualification is highly desirable.Significant experience in Transaction Advisory, PPP, or Commercial Advisory, gained within a leading consultancy, advisory firm, or financial institution.Strong, demonstrable experience in the KSA market, including a clear understanding of government structures, procurement regulations, and the NCP PPP process/Framework.A proven track record delivering PPP, privatization, or investment transactions across municipal, infrastructure, or public‑service sectors in KSA and/or the GCC.Deep understanding of project delivery models, operating models, funding and financing structures, and risk allocation in public‑sector transactions.Established relationships with government entities, NCP, municipalities, investors, developers, and operators in the region.Strong leadership and people‑development skills,

The OpportunityCronos Labs is building the infrastructure to collapse every speculative market into one app. Tokenized stocks, crypto perpetuals, prediction markets, spot: one balance, every market, powered by Crypto.com's 150M+ users and regulated infrastructure for tokenized equities.
We need a VP- Engineering to build the trading engine that makes this possible. The core technical mandate is a hybrid onchain Central Limit Order Book (CLOB) and matching engine with institutional-grade risk infrastructure: cross-asset margin, real-time liquidation systems, and a risk engine that can handle correlated drawdowns across tokenized stocks and crypto derivatives simultaneously. This is the single biggest capability gap on the team. Our blockchain and platform engineering is well-staffed and capable. What we lack is the person who has built centralized, high-performance trading infrastructure and can translate that expertise into a hybrid onchain architecture.
This is a greenfield build. You are not inheriting someone else's architecture. The Sidecar Sequencer (Rust, off-chain) handles order matching at <5ms P50 latency and 100K OPS throughput. The Cronos EVM handles settlement and custody. You will own the full technical stack from matching engine to smart contracts to mobile app, with the team across Smart Contracts, Blockchain, DevOps, Platform, QA, Trading, and Inventory.
The Product You Are BuildingCronos App is a mobile-first trading app that lets users:Trade tokenized stocks (NVDA, TSLA, etc.) 24/7 with increased buying powerTrade crypto perpetuals and spotTrade sports and event outcomes (prediction markets) with the ability to exit earlyUse one balance to collateralize positions across all asset classesChoose between "Simple/Fun Mode" (guardrails, plain language) and "Pro Mode" (advanced execution, lowest fees)The product is powered by Crypto.com's regulated infrastructure for tokenized equities and prediction markets and built on the Cronos blockchain.

Unified margin where tokenized equities can margin crypto trades and vice versa. Real-time risk calculations across stocks, crypto, and prediction markets.Liquidation Infrastructure. Design and implement the liquidation engine, auto-deleveraging (ADL) system, and insurance fund architecture. These must survive correlated market crashes, not just normal volatility. The insurance fund must be verifiable onchain with clear deployment rules.Perpetuals & Derivatives Engine. Perpetual futures with funding rate mechanisms, leverage management, and position lifecycle. Phase 1 forks the CDC Exchange matching engine; Phase 2 transitions to a fully onchain Global Order Book.Oracle Integration & Market Data. Manipulation-resistant price feeds for liquidation triggers, mark price computation, and cross-market reference pricing.

Vault, Clearing House, Insurance Fund, and Governance contracts.Platform & Mobile Engineering. Mobile-first consumer app for emerging markets. Embedded wallet with passkey-based authentication. Account abstraction and TEE/verifiable compute for signing. API Gateway and SDK.DevOps & QA. CI/CD, infrastructure reliability, automated testing, performance benchmarking.CDC Integration & Coordination. Coordinate with the Crypto.com tokenization team on shared Confluence/documentation workflows and align timelines across organizations.

review.title: Director, Government Affairs & Policy
review.jd_text:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Director, Government Affairs & Policy

Overview

The Director of Government Affairs & Policy (GAP)– Cybersecurity for the Eastern Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EEMEA) region will champion cyber diplomacy and execute both regional and global engagement strategies to foster a secure, inclusive, and innovative digital economy. This pivotal role will engage with governments and establish public-private partnerships across EEMEA, while also advancing Mastercard’s cybersecurity policy priorities on a global scale. The position requires a deep understanding of regional legislative processes, as well as the intricacies of geopolitics and international relations in one of the world’s most dynamic and diverse regions.

The Director will be responsible for coordinating internal efforts across various regions and business functions and liaising externally with industry stakeholders, trade organizations, governments, and multilateral entities, in alignment with the EEMEA GAP SVP and other GAP Leads within EEMEA. This leader will work closely and coordinate with GAP Cluster Leads to represent Mastercard in bilateral and multilateral cyber dialogues, international forums, and strategic partnerships, ensuring that regional insights from EEMEA contribute to and align with the company’s global cybersecurity strategies.

  Advance Mastercard’s global cybersecurity policy objectives with EEMEA governments and stakeholders, reporting to the EEMEA Regional Lead for Government Affairs & Policy. Contribute to the development and execution of global and region-specific cyber policy strategies that are fully aligned with national and institutional objectives of EEMEA countries. Represent Mastercard in regional and international cyber policy fora and carry out multi-country advocacy campaigns to engage key stakeholders and government officials across EEMEA. Monitor political, legislative, and decision-making processes of countries and multilateral organizations in the EEMEA region, providing timely assessments of implications for policy or business operations. Prepare and deliver briefing documents, messaging points, and background materials for advocacy engagements and policymaker meetings relevant to the EEMEA context. Build and maintain trusted relationships with senior government officials, foreign counterparts, and multilateral organizations operating in EEMEA and beyond. Engage with industry partners, governments, and other stakeholders to build coalitions and strategic public-private partnerships that support Mastercard’s cybersecurity policy goals throughout EEMEA. Collaborate internally at the global, regional, and local levels and across business functions (e.g., Government Affairs & Policy, Regulatory Affairs, Security Solutions, Corporate Security, Privacy, AI & Data Responsibility, Communications, Strategic Growth) to ensure unified messaging and alignment between EEMEA and global strategies.


All About You

The ideal candidate for this position should embody a global mindset and thrive in a multicultural environment, adept at building consensus across borders and cultures. Based in Dubai or Riyadh, the Director must demonstrate flexibility, creativity, and resilience as they navigate the rapid transformation and regulatory complexity characteristic of EEMEA.

 A global team member who can work across multiple time zones and cultures, operating independently and constructively as a collaborative team player. High-energy, self-motivated. Strong analytical approach to problem solving, with an ability to develop creative, actionable solutions to complex challenges, adapt to short deadlines, and work productively under pressure in dynamic commercial and policy environments. Willingness to travel extensively within the EEMEA region and globally as necessary to advance initiatives and represent Mastercard at relevant events. A distinguished track record of achievement in public policy, government relations, and/or related government experience, particularly within EEMEA government institutions or relevant international organizations. Thorough understanding of the political, legislative, and decision-making processes of EEMEA countries and multilateral organizations, with the ability to anticipate regulatory trends and assess their impact on cyber policy. A proven record of accomplishment in developing and advancing effective cybersecurity policy strategies, particularly in emerging markets and fast-chang

review.title: Executive Director, Structured Credit Investment
review.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
review.jd_text:
Job Purpose

As part of GM Investments, the Portfolio Manager will drive the build-out and growth of Private ABS investments, with a focus on SME, consumer, fintech-originated receivables, and forward-flow structures.

The role will expand the structured credit mandate, developing scalable exposure to private ABS, warehouse facilities, forward-flow agreements, and securitization take-outs, primarily targeting senior and super-senior risk (AAA/AA/A) within the Bank’s risk appetite.

Working in close partnership with the Head of Investments and Head of Fixed Income Investments, the Portfolio Manager will originate, structure, and manage proprietary investment frameworks, contributing to diversification, recurring income generation, and long-term growth of structured credit portfolios.

Key Accountabilities

 Construct and actively manage portfolios of private ABS, with exposure to SME lending, consumer finance, fintech-originated receivables, and specialty asset classes. Originate and execute forward-flow transactions with fintech platforms, non-bank lenders, and specialty finance companies. Develop and oversee warehouse, prefunding, and term securitization structures, including transition from private ABS to rated public take-outs where relevant. Design robust investment frameworks for private ABS, ensuring compliance with internal risk appetite, regulatory constraints, and capital treatment. Maintain active dialogue with fintech platforms, originators, servicers, arrangers, and rating agencies to source opportunities and manage asset performance. Implement strong risk monitoring, performance analytics, and P&L attribution frameworks for private and illiquid structured credit portfolios. Integrate macroeconomic, relative value, and structural analysis into investment decision-making. Apply advanced modelling and structuring expertise, including cash-flow modelling, stress testing, and scenario analysis for private ABS. Identify new asset classes (e.g. BNPL, merchant cash advance, trade receivables, digital SME lending) consistent with the Bank’s risk appetite

Specific Accountability

As this mandate will lead to new portfolios and asset classes, the Portfolio Manager will:

 Build the end-to-end investment and risk infrastructure (credit, legal, operational, servicing). Establish private ABS and forward-flow governance frameworks within the Bank. Act as a center of expertise on private structured credit, supporting Risk, Legal, Compliance, Treasury, and Finance teams. Ensure scalability from bilateral / private transactions to securitized formats. Maintain strong market visibility and represent the Bank across global private ABS and fintech credit ecosystems.Collaborate with internal teams on: Capital efficiency and regulatory treatment, Liquidity management, Accounting and valuation of private assets

Qualifications & Experience

Minimum Qualification

Minimum 10 years of experience in structured credit, private ABS, or specialty finance

Proven track record in managing portfolios and launching new mandates across structured credit products.

Advanced structuring and modelling skills (cash-flow models, stress tests, waterfall analysis).

Experience Gained At

Alternative Asset Managers, Investment Banks, or Specialty Finance Platforms.

Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills for global market engagement
review.verdict_reason: C-suite seniority and FinTech/banking domain match Ahmed's background, but structured credit investment is a specialized niche far removed from DT/PMO core competencies

review.jd_text:
Together we make the difference

We are a leading provider of innovative and high-performance solutions for airport logistics. Our portfolio includes products and solutions for baggage and cargo handling, high-end software for the digitalization of logistics processes as well as an extensive range of maintenance and services. Major customers include renowned airports and airlines around the globe.

We are looking for an experienced Director of Project Execution who will report directly to the Executive Management and oversee multiple airport BHS and MHS projects. You will provide guidance and leadership to the Project Execution and Site Management teams, foster collaboration across internal functions and external stakeholders, and ensure projects are delivered safely, on-time, on-budget, contract compliant, and to the required quality through to handover.

What are my responsibilities?

Lead end-to-end delivery of airport BHS/MHS programs, ensuring safe, on-budget, contract-compliant execution.Serve as senior client and stakeholder lead, aligning airport authorities/operators, consultants/PMCs, contractors, and internal teams.Own the full lifecycle from engineering coordination through installation, integration, testing, commissioning, ORAT, and handover to Service.Drive strong project governance (steerco, reviews, workshops), ensuring clear decisions, actions, and follow-up.Manage portfolio performance across multi-site/multi-country matrix teams; control KPIs, risk, change, and escalations, including recovery/turnaround.Ensure effective site execution in live airport environments, including M&E installation, upgrades, brownfield works, and system interface management.Ensure completeness and quality of technical/contract documentation and reporting across the project lifecycle.Lead successful handovers to Life-Cycle Services, ensuring continuity, training, spares, warranty transition, and sign-off.Support Sales & Bid activities to develop opportunities and ensure executable delivery strategies and resourcing inputs.Build and lead high-performing teams through coaching, accountability, and continuous improvement.

What do I need to qualify for the role?

10+ years delivering complex engineering/automation/site projects; strong ownership of cost, schedule, quality, and safety.Proven leadership in multi-stakeholder environments with strong client-facing and executive communication capability.Strong experience in airport BHS or MHS systems including site execution and commissioning; ORAT exposure is a plus.Solid understanding of automation integration environments (mechanical/electrical, PLC/SCADA/controls, interfaces).Strong commercial and contractual discipline: change control, risk/opportunity management, claims awareness.Proficient in project tools (e.g., MS Project/Primavera, MS Office; Jira/Confluence is a plus).Willingness to travel and operate in dynamic airport environments.Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.
